title: lithospermum officinale var. erythrorhizon
intro: Lithospermum officinale var. erythrorhizon, also known as Rubia or Red Root, is a plant-based species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) with roots and leaves that are used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have shown anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lithospermum officinale var. erythrorhizon include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and menstrual disorders, as well as its use as a natural dye. Lithospermum officinale var. erythrorhizon can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional.
